title: Quiz: Bits and Logic
description: Test your understanding of classical bits and logic operations

questions.question: What is the decimal value of the binary number 1101?
What is 1 XOR 1?
questions.explanation: XOR outputs 1 only when inputs are different. 1 XOR 1 = 0 because both inputs are the same.

questions.question: Which gate outputs 1 only when ALL inputs are 1?
questions.explanation: AND outputs 1 only when all inputs are 1. It's the logical 'multiplication' of inputs.

questions.question: A byte contains 10 bits.
questions.explanation: False. A byte contains 8 bits, which can represent 2⁸ = 256 different values.

questions.question: How many different values can 4 bits represent?
questions.explanation: n bits can represent 2ⁿ values. 4 bits = 2⁴ = 16 values (0 to 15).

questions.question: NAND is a universal gate, meaning any Boolean function can be built using only NAND gates.
questions.explanation: True! NAND is universal. You can build NOT, AND, OR, and any other gate using only NAND gates.

questions.question: Which statements about XOR are true? (Select all that apply)

options.text: A XOR B XOR B = A (XOR is self-inverse)

questions.explanation: a) 0 XOR 0 = 0 ✓. b) 1 XOR 1 = 0 (not 1). c) XOR with same value twice returns original ✓. d) XOR = 1 for different inputs ✓.
